mnemonics.title: Deployment Strategies
mnemonics.content: B-C-R: Blue-Green, Canary, Rolling - three main deployment strategies to remember!

mnemonics.title: CI/CD Pipeline Steps
mnemonics.content: B-T-D: Build, Test, Deploy - the three core stages of CI/CD pipeline

mnemonics.title: IaC Tools
mnemonics.content: TAC: Terraform, Ansible, Chef - popular Infrastructure as Code tools

mnemonics.title: DevOps Benefits
mnemonics.content: FASE: Faster delivery, Automated, Standardized, Error reduction - benefits of DevOps/deployment automation

acronyms.usage: Automatically merging and testing code changes from multiple developers
Automating the release process to production environment
Managing infrastructure using version-controlled code files
Distributing deployment content globally for faster access
Secure protocol for remote server access during deployment

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two identical rooms (blue and green). Guests (traffic) move from one room to another instantly when needed. The standby room becomes active.
Think of a canary in a coal mine - testing safety first. Small group (canary) gets new version first; if healthy, others follow gradually.
Like replacing links in a chain - one by one, maintaining connectivity throughout. No complete shutdown needed.
Assembly line in factory: code enters one end (commit), goes through quality checks (tests), and exits as product (deployment).

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant kitchen using automated recipes - same dish every time, no manual mistakes, faster service.
Like shipping containers in logistics - standardized boxes with everything inside, loaded anywhere without special handling.
Like throwing away and replacing a broken phone instead of repairing it - you get a fresh, known-good state every time.
Like a water treatment plant - water (code) flows through multiple filters (tests) at each stage, becoming cleaner (more reliable) before reaching users.
